description: Output of the MCQ Generator pipeline. Triggered when user requests practice questions for a topic.

questions.question: string — clear, unambiguous question stem
questions.explanation: string — explains WHY the correct answer is right AND why common wrong answers are wrong
questions.concept_tested: string — the specific concept this question probes

questions.question: In Fourier's Law of heat conduction (Q = -kA dT/dx), the negative sign indicates:

options.A: Heat flows from cold to hot regions
options.B: Heat flows in the direction of decreasing temperature gradient
options.D: The temperature gradient is always positive

questions.explanation: The negative sign in Fourier's Law ensures that heat flow Q is positive when the temperature gradient dT/dx is negative (i.e., temperature decreases in the direction of heat flow). Heat always flows from hot to cold — option A has this backwards. Option C is incorrect; k is always a positive material property.
questions.concept_tested: Physical meaning of the negative sign in Fourier's Law

questions.question: Which material has the highest thermal conductivity among the following?
questions.explanation: Copper has a thermal conductivity of approximately 400 W/m·K, making it an excellent heat conductor. Glass wool (~0.04 W/m·K), brick (~0.7 W/m·K), and concrete (~1.4 W/m·K) are all much poorer conductors and are often used as insulators or structural materials.
questions.concept_tested: Thermal conductivity values of common engineering materials

validation_rules: questions array length must equal total_questions
each question must have exactly 4 options: A, B, C, D
correct field must be one of: A, B, C, D
explanation must address both why correct is right and address at least 1 distractor
options must be plausible — no obviously absurd wrong answers
question stem must not give away the answer
difficulty distribution in mixed mode: ~30% easy, ~50% medium, ~20% hard
